sections_added: Architecture component map with flow diagram
macOS IOKit power notifications (dedicated OS thread design)
Network error detection patterns (14 patterns in adapters, 28 in dispatcher)
Transient failure auto-retry logic with cooldown
Retry count scope and reset semantics
file_insights.diagram_types: ASCII architecture maps, state machines, CFRunLoop flow, retry flowchart

patterns_discovered.detail: IOKit notifications require dedicated std::thread, not Tokio task, to meet 8-second IOAllowPowerChange deadline
Exit code 75 (EX_TEMPFAIL) signals transient network failures; retry up to 3 times with 30s cooldown; exit 1+ moves to failed/
Both adapter layer (14 patterns: 'connection refused', 'ssl connection', etc.) and dispatcher layer (28 patterns) detect transient failures via stderr inspection
Card retry count increments only once per episode; resets to 0 on success; saturating_add prevents overflow
Adapter detects and signals via exit code 75; dispatcher also checks failure_reason field with is_network_failure() for redundancy

gotchas_discovered.detail: If IOAllowPowerChange is not called within ~8 seconds of kIOMessageSystemWillSleep, macOS kernel hangs the entire sleep transition for 30 seconds. This is a hard deadline that cannot be violated. Future enhancement: add deadline timer that calls IOAllowPowerChange anyway with warning if pause_all_running() exceeds 8 seconds.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Dedicated OS thread ensures CFRunLoop is not starved by Tokio task scheduler

gotchas_discovered.detail: If adapter makes HTTP request during system sleep, connection will be aborted. On wake, adapter may exit with EAGAIN or ECONNREFUSED. These must be caught and retried, not treated as logic errors.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Network pattern matching at both adapter and dispatcher layers; retry count scope ensures max 3 retries per episode

gotchas_discovered.detail: IP address changes during handoff; DNS may fail for 2-5 seconds; existing TCP connections break. Not documented yet but pattern-matched as transient.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: TCP pre-check with 2s timeout in connectivity probe; retry heuristics sufficient for interim

gotchas_discovered.detail: Retry count stored in meta.json but not persisted in events.jsonl append-only log. If dispatcher crashes before retry count reaches meta.json, next dispatcher restart will not know retry history.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Not currently mitigated; future enhancement: write retry_count to events.jsonl before moving card

approach_outcome.objective: Write output/result.md documenting sleep/wake flow and network resilience
key_deliverables: Comprehensive architectural overview with component diagrams
macOS IOKit + Linux D-Bus implementations documented with code-level details
Sleep/Wake state machine with 5-state lifecycle fully specified
Network error detection patterns extracted and enumerated (14 + 28 patterns)
Retry logic semantics clearly defined (max 3 retries, 30s cooldown, exit code conventions)
architectural_decisions_captured: OS thread vs Tokio task distinction for IOKit
Exit code 75 convention for transient failures
Retry count scope (per-card, per-episode, resets on success)

recommendations.recommendation: Add edge case documentation: What happens if WiFi → cellular switch occurs mid-HTTP-request? Include concrete error logs and retry sequence example.
recommendations.rationale: Currently pattern-matched as transient but not explicitly explained; developers need clear mental model

recommendations.recommendation: Add metrics collection points: count sleep/wake transitions, measure pause_all_running() latency, track retry count distribution, record network error patterns seen in production
recommendations.rationale: 8-second deadline and retry limits are tuned via heuristics; real-world data needed to validate assumptions

recommendations.recommendation: Implement deadline timer enforcement: if pause_all_running() exceeds 6 seconds, log warning and call IOAllowPowerChange anyway; measure actual latency to validate 8-second margin
recommendations.rationale: Current design relies on stub; full implementation needed to handle overload scenarios

recommendations.recommendation: Persist retry_count to events.jsonl append-only log (with timestamp) before moving card to pending/; reconstruct state on dispatcher startup
recommendations.rationale: Prevents loss of retry history if dispatcher crashes between retry decision and meta.json write

recommendations.recommendation: Add harness test: simulate 3-card workload with WiFi disconnect mid-adapter, measure time for re-dispatch on reconnect, verify no cards land in failed/
recommendations.rationale: Network resilience claims need integration test validation; currently pattern-matched but not end-to-end tested

recommendations.recommendation: Add sequence diagram for full retry path: adapter exit → dispatcher detects exit code 75 → increments retry_count → moves to pending/ → cooldown → re-dispatch on next scan_timer tick
recommendations.rationale: Text description is thorough but a sequence diagram would clarify timing and state transitions for visual learners
